title: Adaptive / CDC Shock Absorber Coding and Reset
description: Codes and calibrates adaptive dampers after shock replacement on vehicles with CDC/EDC/MagneRide systems.
common_symptoms: suspension warning after adaptive shock replacement
ride too stiff/soft after damper install
physical_solution: Install correct adaptive damper part number, then code/calibrate damper module so ECU recognizes component and applies correct damping maps.

technical_solution.elm327_feasibility: Limited — coding generally requires OEM tools (ISTA/ODIS/GDS2/FDRS). ELM327 can often read DTCs but cannot complete secure coding on modern platforms.
preconditions: Correct OEM or equivalent adaptive shock installed
Vehicle at curb weight on level surface

procedure_steps.description: Verify damper type matches vehicle option code (CDC/EDC/MagneRide variant). Wrong part causes immediate coding mismatch.
Read suspension module DTCs and identify coding-related faults before calibration.
Run OEM coding/calibration routine (ISTA for BMW, ODIS for VW/Audi, GDS2 for GM, FDRS/IDS for Ford).
If supported, execute damper adaptation reset via $31 routine and store coding values via $2E.
Road test over low/high frequency bumps and verify no warning lights, balanced damping response, and no return DTCs.

oem_notes.Toyota/Lexus: Adaptive Variable Suspension on select Lexus models may need Techstream calibration after replacement.
oem_notes.Honda/Acura: Limited adaptive damper coding; most replacements are straightforward. SH-AWD adaptive variants may need HDS checks.
oem_notes.Nissan/Infiniti: Electronic damping on select Infiniti models, CONSULT preferred for calibration.
oem_notes.Ford: MagneRide-equipped models require IDS/FDRS service functions after replacement.
oem_notes.GM: MagneRide/Continuous Damping Control often requires GDS2 relearn and calibration.
oem_notes.BMW: EDC (Electronic Damper Control) coding via ISTA is standard on adaptive suspension replacements.
oem_notes.VW/Audi: DCC/CDC coding typically requires ODIS, with SFD restrictions on newer platforms.
oem_notes.Stellantis: Adaptive damping on select performance trims requires wiTECH routines.
oem_notes.Hyundai/Kia: Electronically controlled suspension on some Genesis platforms may require GDS2 adaptation.
